original_text: Credit risk is inherent to American Express's business model as the company provides charge and credit payment card products to consumers, small businesses, mid-sized companies, and large corporations worldwide. As a credit services provider, the company is exposed to potential loan losses and charge-offs that could impact profitability during economic downturns. The Q3 2025 net income of $2,902,000,000 represents a 27.86% net margin, but this profitability could be pressured if credit quality deteriorates. Specific credit loss metrics and charge-off rates are not available in the provided evidence to quantify this risk more precisely.
revised_text: American Express operates in the Financial - Credit Services industry and provides charge and credit payment card products and travel-related services worldwide. The company operates through Global Consumer Services Group and Global Commercial Services segments. In Q3 2025, American Express reported net income of $2,902,000,000, representing a net margin of 27.86%.
changes_summary: Removed unsupported claims regarding credit risk exposure, loan losses, charge-offs, and economic downturn impacts as no provided evidence chunks contained data on these metrics (addressing Issue 1). Retained business model description and Q3 2025 financial performance data which are substantiated by the available company profile and income statement chunks.
